parameters.description: Number which identifies the transaction document. It is unique for the respective business transaction category.

parameters.description: Shortened Item Number in Document
parameters.name: ServiceConfirmationItem

parameters.description:
The language key indicates  
- the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.
parameters.name: Language

parameters.description:
The text ID defines the various types of texts related to a text object. For example, the object "TEXT" (standard texts) can have the following text IDs:  
ST for user-specific standard texts (individual texts)SYST for cross-application system textsvarious IDs for specific application departments. You must have the appropriate access authorization in order to access these texts.
parameters.name: LongTextID

API_SERVICE_CONFIRMATION_SRV.A_ServiceCo.title: Price Element of Srvc Confirmation Item

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

PricingProcedureStep.title: Number that determines the sequence of the condition within a procedure.

PricingProcedureCounter.title: Access number of the conditions within a step in the pricing procedure.
PricingProcedureCounter.description: During automatic pricing, the system takes into account the sequence specified by the counter.

ConditionType.title: The key that uniquely identifies the condition.
ConditionType.description: The condition type indicates, for example, whether, during pricing, the system applies a price, a discount, a surcharge, or other pricing elements, such as freight costs and sales taxes. For each of these pricing elements, there is a condition type defined in the system.

ConditionRateValue.title: Determines how the system calculates prices using a specific condition.
ConditionRateValue.description: Depending on the condition type, it can be a fixed amount, a percentage, or per mille. If the condition includes a pricing scale, the condition amount or percentage displays the first line of the scale.The following examples illustrate how you can use the condition amount or percentage in different condition types:If you create a condition that includes prices (for example, prices for a material), you enter an amount.If you create a condition based on percentage, such as discounts or surcharges (for example, a customer-specific discount), you enter the value of a percentage. If the condition is a discount, the system automatically enters a minus sign behind the amount and a percent sign in the Condition Unit field.

ConditionCurrency.title: Currency key for amounts in the system.

ConditionQuantity.title: The quantity on which the amount or percentage is based. The quantity is expressed in sales units (for example, bottles).
ConditionQuantity.description: You want to create a condition record for a material price of US$8 for 1 piece. You enter "8" in the Rate field, "USD" in the Unit field, and "1" as the condition pricing unit in this field.You create a condition record that gives a fixed discount of $10 to a customer who buys 1000 bottles. In this case, the condition pricing unit is 1000.

ConditionQuantityUnit.title: Quantity unit, to which the condition amount or percentage refers.

API_SERVICE_CONFIRMATION_SRV.A_ServiceCo.title: Reference Obj. of Srvc Confirmation Item

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceReferenceEquipment.title: Number by which a piece of equipment can be clearly identified.

ServiceRefFunctionalLocation.title: Label that uniquely identifies a functional location.
ServiceRefFunctionalLocation.description: If alternative labels exist for a functional location, you can define in your user profile which label should be displayed in this field.

API_SERVICE_CONFIRMATION_SRV.A_ServiceCo.title: Reference Obj. of Srvc Confirmation Item (for create)

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceRefFunctionalLocation.title: Label that uniquely identifies a functional location.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

PersonResponsible.title: Key identifying a business partner in the SAP system. The key is unique within a client.

CustMgmtPartnerIsMainPartner.title: Specifies the main partner in a group of partners with the same partner function.
CustMgmtPartnerIsMainPartner.description: When exactly one partner with this partner function is required, in partner determination for example, the system chooses the main partner.

Language.title: The language key indicates
Language.description: - the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.

LongTextID.title: The text ID defines the various types of texts related to a text object. For example, the object "TEXT" (standard texts) can have the following text IDs:
LongTextID.description: ST for user-specific standard texts (individual texts)SYST for cross-application system textsvarious IDs for specific application departments. You must have the appropriate access authorization in order to access these texts.

API_SERVICE_CONFIRMATION_SRV.A_ServiceCo.title: Text of Service Confirmation Item (for update)

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationItemUUID.title: The unique ID for an operation is a technical key by which the operation can be uniquely identified.
ServiceConfirmationItemUUID.description: This key is only used internally, and the operation number is always displayed on the interface. However, the operation number is not necessarily unique.

ServiceObjectType.title: Determines the business context in which a transaction type or item category can be used (for example, Service, Sales, Activity).
ServiceObjectType.description: You assign one or more business transaction categories to a transaction type.Depending on the leading business transaction category, only specific combinations are possible with other business transaction categories. The business transaction category Sales can only be combined with Activities, for example.

ServiceDocumentItemObjectType.title: Specifies the business context of an item category.

Language.title: Short Text Language for an Item

Product.title: Product that the customer originally ordered.
Product.description: If, for whatever reason, you have to deliver a substitute product for the one originally required, you can see which product the customer originally ordered.

Quantity.title: Total (rounded) order quantity in sales units of measure for this item.
Quantity.description: This field is only filled for a sales or opportunity item. This field remains empty for other item object types.

QuantityUnit.title: Unit of measure in which the product is sold.

ServiceConfItemCategory.title: Key that identifies an item category.
ServiceConfItemCategory.description: An item category defines the characteristics and attributes of a transaction item and thus controls the processing of an item.

ExecutingServiceEmployee.title: The 32-digit GUID for identifying a service employee.

ServicePerformer.title: Alphanumeric key that identifies a partner in the system.
ServicePerformer.description: The system identifies a partner number in relation to the number type.

BillableControl.title: Specifies the ID of an accounting indicator that enables you to control the following for products that you do not want fully bill for:
BillableControl.description: PricingDifferentiation of actual costs in controllingIf you want to post costs in an integrated Controlling system using the accounting indicator as a key, you need to ensure that the accounting indicators that you create in this step are identical to the accounting indicators in the SAP system.For services performed in goodwill, you want to offer a discount of 50%. You also want to track costs for goodwill services in controlling.

ReferenceServiceOrder.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

TimeSheetOvertimeCategory.title: Overtime is defined as the time an employee works outside the regular working times. Overtime Category is the classification of the overtime hours.
TimeSheetOvertimeCategory.description: It is used in the Manage My Timesheet app to efficiently create time entries.

RespyMgmtGlobalTeamID.title: Global Team ID in Responsibility Mgmt.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationItemUUID.title: The unique ID for an operation is a technical key by which the operation can be uniquely identified.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number which identifies the transaction document. It is unique for the respective business transaction category.

Language.title: The language key indicates
Language.description: - the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.

LongTextID.title: The text ID defines the various types of texts related to a text object. For example, the object "TEXT" (standard texts) can have the following text IDs:
LongTextID.description: ST for user-specific standard texts (individual texts)SYST for cross-application system textsvarious IDs for specific application departments. You must have the appropriate access authorization in order to access these texts.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number which identifies the transaction document. It is unique for the respective business transaction category.

Language.title: The language key indicates
Language.description: - the language in which texts are displayed,- the language in which you enter texts,- the language in which the system prints texts.

LongTextID.title: The text ID defines the various types of texts related to a text object. For example, the object "TEXT" (standard texts) can have the following text IDs:
LongTextID.description: ST for user-specific standard texts (individual texts)SYST for cross-application system textsvarious IDs for specific application departments. You must have the appropriate access authorization in order to access these texts.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationType.title: Key that identifies a transaction type.
ServiceConfirmationType.description: A transaction type defines the characteristics and attributes of a business transaction (for example, sales order, service request, visit), and determines the controlling attributes (for example, text determination procedure, partner determination procedure, status profile, organizational data profile). A transaction type controls the processing of a specific business transaction.

ServiceConfirmationUUID.title: The unique ID for an operation is a technical key by which the operation can be uniquely identified.
ServiceConfirmationUUID.description: This key is only used internally, and the operation number is always displayed on the interface. However, the operation number is not necessarily unique.

ServiceConfirmationDescription.title: Short description for a transaction.
ServiceConfirmationDescription.description: The description can be used during a search for the transaction.

ServiceObjectType.title: Determines the business context in which a transaction type or item category can be used (for example, Service, Sales, Activity).
ServiceObjectType.description: You assign one or more business transaction categories to a transaction type.Depending on the leading business transaction category, only specific combinations are possible with other business transaction categories. The business transaction category Sales can only be combined with Activities, for example.

Language.title: Language in which the transaction short text was entered.

ServiceDocumentPriority.title: Enter the priority of the document, for example, high or low.

PurchaseOrderByCustomer.title: Number used by the sold-to party to uniquely identify their sales document (such as an order).
PurchaseOrderByCustomer.description: The number represents the connection between the order from the sold-to party and the sales document generated. Customers can use these numbers in correspondence to help determine specific document information. If a number is used for an order, then you can also print out this number on documents sent to customers (such as the delivery note).

CustomerPurchaseOrderDate.title: Date which appears on the customer purchase order. For example, it can describe the day on which the customer created the purchase order.

SalesOrganization.title: An organizational unit responsible for the sale of certain products or services. The responsibility of a sales organization may include legal liability for products and customer claims.
SalesOrganization.description: You can assign any number of distribution channels and divisions to a sales organization. A particular combination of sales organization, distribution channel, and division is known as a sales area.

DistributionChannel.title: Channel through which the goods or services reach the customer. Typical examples of distribution channels are wholesale trade, retail trade, or direct sales.
DistributionChannel.description: You can define data for business partners and products per sales organization and distribution channel. In a sales organization you can deliver to a specific customer through several distribution channels.You can assign a specific distribution channel as an attribute to one or more organizational units. These can be sales organizations or subordinate organizational units for a sales organization such as sales offices.If you work with a selection of sales offices from one sales organization, each of these sales offices can be responsible for a specific distribution channel (for example, wholesale trade, retail trade, direct sales). The attribute "distribution channel" is then assigned to each sales office.When determining the sales area, the system reads for which sales organization an office with a specific distribution channel is assigned and uses this to determine the combination of sales organization and distribution channel.

Division.title: Option of grouping products.
Division.description: A division can be used, for example, to display a specific product group.

SalesOffice.title: A physical location (for example, a branch office) that has responsibility for the sale of certain products or services within a given geographical area.
SalesOffice.description: When you create sales statistics, you can use a sales office as one of the selection criteria. When you print out order confirmations, you can include the address of the sales office.You can assign each customer to a sales office in the customer master record.Within a sales office you can establish sales groups (for example, departments) with specific sales responsibilities. Each person who works in the sales office can be assigned to a sales group in his or her user master record. Each customer can also be assigned to a particular sales group in the customer master record.

SalesGroup.title: A group of sales people who are responsible for processing sales of certain products or services.
SalesGroup.description: By using sales groups you can designate different areas of responsibility within a sales office.  When you generate sales statistics, you can use the sales group as one of the selection criteria.If sales office personnel service both retail and wholesale markets, you can assign a sales group to each market.You assign each salesperson to a sales group in his or her user master record. You assign each customer to a particular sales group in the customer's master record.

SoldToParty.title: The 32-digit GUID used to identify the sold-to party.

ShipToParty.title: Specifies the ship-to party as a 32-digit GUID.

BillToParty.title: The 32 digit GUID for the bill-to party.

PayerParty.title: The 32-digit GUID that specifies the payer.

ReferenceServiceOrder.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationIsFinal.title: Final service confirmation for the related service order

TransactionCurrency.title: Currency in which the settlement is carried out with the customer or the vendor.
TransactionCurrency.description: The currency is maintained per sales area in the business partner master. The currency for the customer is created from the business partner master in the document. You can change the currency as long as no follow-up functions (for example, billing document) have been triggered.

RespyMgmtGlobalTeamID.title: Global Team ID in Responsibility Mgmt.

SalesOrganizationOrgUnitID.title: Organizational unit responsible for specific products or services for sales and distribution. The responsibility of a sales organization can include legal liability for products and the customer's right of recourse.
SalesOrganizationOrgUnitID.description: You can assign as many distribution channels and divisions as you want to a sales organization. A specific combination of sales organizations, distribution channels and divisions is known as a  sales area.

SalesOfficeOrgUnitID.title: Location (for example, branch office), that is responsible for specific sales and distribution products and services in a particular geographic area.
SalesOfficeOrgUnitID.description: You can form purchasing groups (for example, departments) in a sales office with specific sales responsibilities.

SalesGroupOrgUnitID.title: Group of sales employees, responsible for processing specific products or services.
SalesGroupOrgUnitID.description: You can use purchasing groups to form different areas of responsibility in a sales office.

ServiceConfirmation.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationType.title: Key that identifies a transaction type.
ServiceConfirmationType.description: A transaction type defines the characteristics and attributes of a business transaction (for example, sales order, service request, visit), and determines the controlling attributes (for example, text determination procedure, partner determination procedure, status profile, organizational data profile). A transaction type controls the processing of a specific business transaction.

ServiceConfirmationUUID.title: The unique ID for an operation is a technical key by which the operation can be uniquely identified.
ServiceConfirmationUUID.description: This key is only used internally, and the operation number is always displayed on the interface. However, the operation number is not necessarily unique.

ServiceConfirmationDescription.title: Short description for a transaction.
ServiceConfirmationDescription.description: The description can be used during a search for the transaction.

ServiceObjectType.title: Determines the business context in which a transaction type or item category can be used (for example, Service, Sales, Activity).
ServiceObjectType.description: You assign one or more business transaction categories to a transaction type.Depending on the leading business transaction category, only specific combinations are possible with other business transaction categories. The business transaction category Sales can only be combined with Activities, for example.

Language.title: Language in which the transaction short text was entered.

ServiceDocumentPriority.title: Enter the priority of the document, for example, high or low.

PurchaseOrderByCustomer.title: Number used by the sold-to party to uniquely identify their sales document (such as an order).
PurchaseOrderByCustomer.description: The number represents the connection between the order from the sold-to party and the sales document generated. Customers can use these numbers in correspondence to help determine specific document information. If a number is used for an order, then you can also print out this number on documents sent to customers (such as the delivery note).

CustomerPurchaseOrderDate.title: Date which appears on the customer purchase order. For example, it can describe the day on which the customer created the purchase order.

SalesOrganization.title: An organizational unit responsible for the sale of certain products or services. The responsibility of a sales organization may include legal liability for products and customer claims.
SalesOrganization.description: You can assign any number of distribution channels and divisions to a sales organization. A particular combination of sales organization, distribution channel, and division is known as a sales area.

DistributionChannel.title: Channel through which the goods or services reach the customer. Typical examples of distribution channels are wholesale trade, retail trade, or direct sales.
DistributionChannel.description: You can define data for business partners and products per sales organization and distribution channel. In a sales organization you can deliver to a specific customer through several distribution channels.You can assign a specific distribution channel as an attribute to one or more organizational units. These can be sales organizations or subordinate organizational units for a sales organization such as sales offices.If you work with a selection of sales offices from one sales organization, each of these sales offices can be responsible for a specific distribution channel (for example, wholesale trade, retail trade, direct sales). The attribute "distribution channel" is then assigned to each sales office.When determining the sales area, the system reads for which sales organization an office with a specific distribution channel is assigned and uses this to determine the combination of sales organization and distribution channel.

Division.title: Option of grouping products.
Division.description: A division can be used, for example, to display a specific product group.

SalesOffice.title: A physical location (for example, a branch office) that has responsibility for the sale of certain products or services within a given geographical area.
SalesOffice.description: When you create sales statistics, you can use a sales office as one of the selection criteria. When you print out order confirmations, you can include the address of the sales office.You can assign each customer to a sales office in the customer master record.Within a sales office you can establish sales groups (for example, departments) with specific sales responsibilities. Each person who works in the sales office can be assigned to a sales group in his or her user master record. Each customer can also be assigned to a particular sales group in the customer master record.

SalesGroup.title: A group of sales people who are responsible for processing sales of certain products or services.
SalesGroup.description: By using sales groups you can designate different areas of responsibility within a sales office.  When you generate sales statistics, you can use the sales group as one of the selection criteria.If sales office personnel service both retail and wholesale markets, you can assign a sales group to each market.You assign each salesperson to a sales group in his or her user master record. You assign each customer to a particular sales group in the customer's master record.

SoldToParty.title: The 32-digit GUID used to identify the sold-to party.

ShipToParty.title: Specifies the ship-to party as a 32-digit GUID.

BillToParty.title: The 32 digit GUID for the bill-to party.

PayerParty.title: The 32-digit GUID that specifies the payer.

ReferenceServiceOrder.title: Number that identifies the transaction document. It is unique for each business transaction category.

ServiceConfirmationIsFinal.title: Final service confirmation for the related service order

TransactionCurrency.title: Currency in which the settlement is carried out with the customer or the vendor.
TransactionCurrency.description: The currency is maintained per sales area in the business partner master. The currency for the customer is created from the business partner master in the document. You can change the currency as long as no follow-up functions (for example, billing document) have been triggered.

RespyMgmtGlobalTeamID.title: Global Team ID in Responsibility Mgmt.

SalesOrganizationOrgUnitID.title: Organizational unit responsible for specific products or services for sales and distribution. The responsibility of a sales organization can include legal liability for products and the customer's right of recourse.
SalesOrganizationOrgUnitID.description: You can assign as many distribution channels and divisions as you want to a sales organization. A specific combination of sales organizations, distribution channels and divisions is known as a  sales area.

SalesOfficeOrgUnitID.title: Location (for example, branch office), that is responsible for specific sales and distribution products and services in a particular geographic area.
SalesOfficeOrgUnitID.description: You can form purchasing groups (for example, departments) in a sales office with specific sales responsibilities.

SalesGroupOrgUnitID.title: Group of sales employees, responsible for processing specific products or services.
SalesGroupOrgUnitID.description: You can use purchasing groups to form different areas of responsibility in a sales office.

ServiceConfirmationType.title: Key that identifies a transaction type.
ServiceConfirmationType.description: A transaction type defines the characteristics and attributes of a business transaction (for example, sales order, service request, visit), and determines the controlling attributes (for example, text determination procedure, partner determination procedure, status profile, organizational data profile). A transaction type controls the processing of a specific business transaction.

ServiceConfirmationUUID.description: This key is only used internally, and the operation number is always displayed on the interface. However, the operation number is not necessarily unique.

ServiceObjectType.description: You assign one or more business transaction categories to a transaction type.Depending on the leading business transaction category, only specific combinations are possible with other business transaction categories. The business transaction category Sales can only be combined with Activities, for example.

count.description: The number of entities in the collection. Available when using the $inlinecount query option, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/).

innererror.description: The structure of this object is service-specific

top.name: $top
top.description: Show only the first n items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

skip.name: $skip
skip.description: Skip the first n items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

count.name: $inlinecount
count.description: Include count of items, see [URI Conventions (OData Version 2.0)](https://www.odata.org/documentation/odata-version-2-0/uri-conventions/)

apply.name: $apply
apply.description: Apply basic grouping and aggregation functionality, see [Aggregation](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata-data-aggregation-ext/v4.0/odata-data-aggregation-ext-v4.0.html)
